raw_review_excerpt:
The current artifact is internally coherent and substantially freeze-ready. It resolves the earlier placeholder-command and ambiguous-semantics issues, but one material verification gap remains: stale-gate rejection is specified for both approve and reject paths, while the frozen end-gate verify commands only prove stale-token rejection on approve.

Issues:
- 1. [WARN] Reject stale-gate behavior is specified but not end-gate-proven symmetrically
closure_verification: The carried content fixes from earlier rounds are present in the current artifact. The remaining unproven item is the artifact's own repo-browser preflight claim, which was not independently revalidated here.

prior_round_closure.title: P99 verify commands replaced placeholders with exact commands
prior_round_closure.caller_claim: Cycle 1 said the plan now freezes exact P99 CLI and API verify commands.
prior_round_closure.verification_notes: Current artifact includes explicit `bun test ... -t` commands in both P99-T1 and P99-T2.

prior_round_closure.title: Approve/reject JSON semantics clarified under auto-resume
prior_round_closure.caller_claim: Cycle 1 said approve/reject JSON semantics were made explicit.
prior_round_closure.verification_notes: Current artifact now freezes Decision E and provides concrete approve/reject JSON examples, including post-resume and partial-success behavior.

prior_round_closure.title: API gap wording narrowed to raw-readback-vs-canonical-snapshot
prior_round_closure.caller_claim: Cycle 1 said the plan no longer overstates API absence.
prior_round_closure.verification_notes: The artifact now explicitly says `GET /api/workflows/runs/:runId` already exists and frames the v1 gap as lack of canonical `HumanGateSnapshot` semantics.

prior_round_closure.title: Partial-success resume-launch-failed branch explicitly verified
prior_round_closure.caller_claim: Cycle 2 said the partial-success branch was given explicit verify hooks.
prior_round_closure.verification_notes: Current artifact includes a concrete JSON example plus a dedicated CLI verify command and exit-criteria mention in P99-T1.

prior_round_closure.title: Legacy paused-run compatibility has an explicit end-gate verify hook
prior_round_closure.caller_claim: Cycle 2 said legacy compatibility now has frozen verification.
prior_round_closure.verification_notes: Current artifact includes legacy compatibility in P1 foundation tests and a dedicated `workflow show --json` compatibility verify command in P99-T1.

prior_round_closure.title: Repo-browser preflight rechecked runtime anchors and test homes
prior_round_closure.caller_claim: Cycle 4 said the cited runtime anchors and server test-file references were verified against repo reality.
prior_round_closure.verification_notes: The current artifact records those assertions in the Repo Browser Preflight section, but this review did not independently inspect the repo files.

review.summary: The current artifact is internally coherent and substantially freeze-ready. It resolves the earlier placeholder-command and ambiguous-semantics issues, but one material verification gap remains: stale-gate rejection is specified for both approve and reject paths, while the frozen end-gate verify commands only prove stale-token rejection on approve.

rubric_cards.title: Claim anchoring
rubric_cards.claim: Most load-bearing claims are anchored in the current plan artifact, especially the verified-current-state, contract-freeze, P99 verify-command, and acceptance-criteria sections.
rubric_cards.evidence: The review used the supplied plan text as the primary source. No cited repo files were independently re-inspected in this review.

rubric_cards.title: Disposition matrix
rubric_cards.claim: Artifact evidence is strong, conversation evidence is limited to the prior-round packet, and campaign evidence is only slice-level.
rubric_cards.evidence: See matrix and coverage fields below.

rubric_cards.title: Coverage scope
rubric_cards.claim: This review covered the current artifact plus prior-round claims embedded in the prompt, not the full campaign or independently inspected repo state.
rubric_cards.evidence: No full thread transcript, no campaign artifacts, and no live repo-file recheck were available under the stated constraints.

issues.title: Reject stale-gate behavior is specified but not end-gate-proven symmetrically
issues.details: The artifact freezes stale-gate semantics across approve/reject paths in P2-T3, P3-T1, and the acceptance criteria, but the frozen P99 verify commands only prove stale-token rejection for `approve`. `reject` is only covered for the no-token compatibility case. That leaves one load-bearing safety behavior specified but not explicitly end-gate-verified.
issues.suggested_fix: Add exact CLI and API verify commands for stale-token rejection on `reject`, or explicitly narrow v1 stale-token enforcement proof to approve-only if that is the actual intended boundary.

verification_gaps: Cited runtime-anchor and test-home claims inside the Repo Browser Preflight section were not independently re-inspected in this review.
Exact `bun test ... -t` names are assessed as frozen artifact intent, not as currently existing passing tests.
No direct repo inspection was performed for `packages/workflows/src/*`, `packages/cli/src/commands/workflow.ts`, or server test files under the imposed constraints.
recommended_fixes: Add symmetric reject stale-token verify commands at the CLI and API end-gates.
If no further artifact revision is planned, record explicitly that repo-grounding still needs a lightweight pre-implementation recheck of the cited files/test homes.
evidence_sources: Prior-round context block in the prompt

review.closure_verification: The carried content fixes from earlier rounds are present in the current artifact. The remaining unproven item is the artifact's own repo-browser preflight claim, which was not independently revalidated here.
workflow_compliance: The artifact has clear phase structure, exit criteria, acceptance criteria, risks, rollout order, and a defined E2E gate.
It explicitly freezes the v1 CLI/API boundary instead of leaving parity semantics implicit.
It now includes exact frozen P99 verify commands rather than placeholders.
One material verify-command gap remains for reject stale-token rejection.
technical_reality: The artifact consistently frames persisted pause metadata as the canonical runtime source and live SSE/chat surfaces as non-canonical observability layers.
The artifact explicitly keeps `GET /api/workflows/runs/:runId` as a raw run-plus-events surface in v1 while making CLI the canonical normalized snapshot surface.
The artifact defines explicit structured handling for 'decision recorded, resume launch failed' rather than treating it as silent success or generic failure.
Repo-grounding assertions in the preflight section remain artifact-asserted rather than independently rechecked in this review.
assumptions_to_verify: Current repo reality still matches the stated server test home `packages/server/src/routes/api.workflow-runs.test.ts`.
Approve and reject paths can share the same expected-gate-token validation semantics without hidden divergence.
Existing workflow-run metadata is sufficient to carry gate token/version without requiring deeper persistence changes.
JSON mode can fully suppress stdout chatter while preserving operator-usable diagnostics off stdout.
verification_sufficiency: Sufficient to judge the current artifact's internal workflow-plan structure and prior-round closure against the supplied text.
Insufficient to independently confirm repo anchors, command availability, and exact test-home reality because no repo files were re-inspected.
Insufficient for full-campaign conclusions because the review covered only the current artifact and prior-round packet.
downstream_risks: Reject-path stale-token handling could ship without explicit regression coverage.
Implementation could start from a stale file/test map if the preflight assertions drifted after the artifact was written.
CLI/API gate semantics could still drift if stale-token validation is not exercised symmetrically across both mutation actions.
review.freeze_recommendation: Safe to freeze. Carry the remaining WARN/NOTE items as explicit follow-ups or implementation-time checks.
